subject: ap-chemistry
title: Solutions & Separation Techniques
stimulus:
A student investigates various dyes using paper chromatography. The student has samples of three pure dyes,
labeled A, B, and C, and an unknown sample that contains one of the three dyes. The student prepares the
chromatography chambers shown above on the left by putting a drop of each dye at the indicated position on
the chromatography paper (a polar material) and standing the paper in a nonpolar solvent. The developed
chromatograms are shown above on the right.

parts.prompt:
Which dye (A, B, or C) is the least polar? Justify your answer in terms of the interactions between the dyes
and the solvent or between the dyes and the paper.
rubric_criteria: Which dye (A, B, or C) is the least polar? Justify your answer in terms of the interactions between the
I point is earned for the correct
Dye C is the least polar because it moved the farthest.
Nonpolar dyes are more strongly attracted to the nonpolar solvent.
Nonpolar dyes are least strongly retained by the polar paper.

parts.prompt:
Which dye is present in the unknown sample? Justify your answer.
